metadata.title: Why Deleting the Race Column Doesn't Delete Race
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Computational Skepticism for AI candidate 25. Source: books/computational-skepticism-for-ai/chapters/07-fairness-metrics-choosing-a-definition-and-defending-it.md (fairness through unawareness, proxy reconstruction, lines 209, 211).
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = fair / legitimate path; CRIMSON = proxy reconstruction / indirect discrimination; GOLD = the key insight / what FTU actually guarantees; SLATE = the deleted column / ghost reconstruction
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no Lipschitz formalism, no impossibility theorem, no causal-path taxonomy, no legal disparate-treatment/impact framing
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B15: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. You deleted the race column. The model still knows.

card.copy:
Why Deleting the Race Column
Doesn't Delete Race

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. You deleted the race column. The model still knows.

beats.narration_text: A loan model. The sensitive attribute — race — is removed from the feature set. Fairness through unawareness: if the model can't see it, it can't use it. But the model still sees zip code. It still sees surname. It still sees occupation. Each of those columns is correlated with race. Together they reconstruct the deleted column — and the discrimination continues through a path you just made invisible to yourself.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A loan model. The sensitive attribute — race — is removed from the feature set. Fairness through unawareness: if the model can't see it, it can't use it. But th

beats.narration_text: If you delete the sensitive attribute — and the model genuinely cannot directly see it — how can the model still use it?

props.headline: If you delete the sensitive attribute — and the model genuinely…

card.copy:
If you delete the sensitive attribute,
how can the model still use it?

beats.narration_text: Sensitive attributes don't live in a single column. They're encoded in the correlated structure of the other columns. Zip code carries neighborhood demographics. Surname carries ancestry signals. Occupation carries historical access patterns. Remove the column — the signal remains in the network of correlations you left in place.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Sensitive attributes don't live in a single column. They're encoded in the correlated structure of the other columns. Zip code carries neighborhood demographics

beats.narration_text: Fairness through unawareness blocks the direct use of the attribute. It does nothing about indirect discrimination through correlated features. The model recovers the attribute from its shadow — and the audit trail says 'race not used' because race, technically, wasn't there to use.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Fairness through unawareness blocks the direct use of the attribute. It does nothing about indirect discrimination through correlated features. The model recove

beats.narration_text: FTU blocks the direct use of an attribute. It does nothing about indirect discrimination.

props.headline: FTU blocks the direct use of an attribute. It does nothing…

beats.image_description: A spreadsheet with columns of data — one column greyed out and crossed through, but the remaining columns casting overlapping shadows that merge into the shape of the deleted column

build.suggested: suggested prompt: FTU blocks the direct use of an attribute. It does nothing about indirect discrimination.

beats.narration_text: Sensitive attributes reconstruct from proxies. Omit race but keep zip code and the model may be using race anyway. The reconstruction happens inside the model's learned associations — not in a column you can point to. Deleting the column removes the direct path. The indirect paths stay open.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Sensitive attributes reconstruct from proxies. Omit race but keep zip code and the model may be using race anyway. The reconstruction happens inside the model's

beats.narration_text: Fairness through unawareness is not a fairness guarantee. It is a documentation choice that looks like one.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Fairness through unawareness is not a fairness guarantee. It is a documentation choice that looks like one.

beats.narration_text: If fairness through unawareness doesn't work, what does? At minimum: audit the proxy features. Do your remaining features correlate with the deleted attribute? If yes, the model can reconstruct the signal. That reconstruction needs to be the thing you measure and constrain — not the presence of the column.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: If fairness through unawareness doesn't work, what does? At minimum: audit the proxy features. Do your remaining features correlate with the deleted attribute?

beats.narration_text: Sensitive attributes reconstruct from proxies — zip code, surname, occupation — that correlate with group membership. Omit race but keep zip code and the model may be using race anyway.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Sensitive attributes reconstruct from proxies — zip code, surname, occupation — that correlate with group membership. Omit race but keep zip code and the model

beats.narration_text: Deleting the column is a documentation move. It tells you what the model cannot directly see. It does not tell you what the model cannot reconstruct. Auditing fairness means measuring outcomes by group — not confirming that the group's column was absent.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Deleting the column is a documentation move. It tells you what the model cannot directly see. It does not tell you what the model cannot reconstruct. Auditing f

beats.narration_text: A hiring model removes candidate names to eliminate name-based bias. The name column is gone. But the model still sees university attended — which correlates with race at nineteen percent. It sees ZIP code of home address — demographic signal. It sees previous employer — industry segregation patterns. Within six months, the hiring rates for Black candidates have not changed. The name was the column. The signal was in the remaining features.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A hiring model removes candidate names to eliminate name-based bias. The name column is gone. But the model still sees university attended — which correlates wi

beats.narration_text: Correlated proxies reconstruct the deleted column inside the model's learned weights. Fairness through unawareness blocks direct access — not indirect reconstruction. Measure outcomes by group. That's the fairness check. The column's absence isn't.

props.headline: Correlated proxies reconstruct the deleted column inside the model's learned weights.…

card.copy:
FTU is a documentation choice
that looks like a fairness guarantee.
card.sub: The attribute reconstructs from proxies. Measure outcomes — not column presence.

beats.name: OutroSeries
